artifacts.content:
Hi Em,

I wanted to write this out instead of trying to cram it into a quick text. My joints have been a mess again, and they’re finally moving ahead with the Krystexxa infusions. Dr. Whitaker at Tulsa Rheumatology wants to keep it at 8 mg IV every 2 weeks and is talking about starting around late January or early February 2026, but I have to juggle it with everything else we’ve got going on.

You remember that scare back in February 2020 when Dr. Singh at St. Francis started me on allopurinol 300 mg a day? Ten days in I broke out in that awful itchy rash, my face swelled, and they kept me overnight in the ER. He called it a “possible Stevens‑Johnson precursor.” To make it worse, Grandma Karen was on the same floor for her CHF flare, so Laura was running between our rooms. Ever since then, anything new with IVs or meds makes me a little jumpy.

Then in August 2024 Noah had that anaphylactic reaction to peanuts at kindergarten in OKC and spent two nights at OU Children’s. After that, when we talked on August 28 you made me promise not to go to these Krystexxa infusions alone. So Laura’s planning to come with me to each one and wants to be copied on any last‑minute schedule changes.

On top of that, we’re still paying on the Saint Francis ER bill from when Mom visited last October and panicked when she saw me crawling to the bathroom during a flare. That’s the $1,900 we put on a 12‑month payment plan. Between that, your mom’s teeth, and regular bills, we’re watching every dollar.

Anyway, I’ll send you the dates once the infusion center calls. Tell Noah and Mia Grandpa’s doing all this so he can still get down on the floor with them—just maybe a little slower.

Love,
Dad

metadata.subject: Infusions, allergies, and why Laura’s coming with me

artifacts.content:
Laura,

I’m still wound up from that meeting with Carl this morning, so I’m writing it down before I forget the details. He pulled me into his office and went over my attendance since October. Two write‑ups already for missing the 5:00 AM start after those nights when the gout in my toes and ankles was so bad I could barely stand. Today, November 12, he basically said one more absence like that and I could lose the supervisor job at GreenCountry.

It feels like this has been building for a while. Remember back on December 18 last year when Mark Delaney slapped that 60‑day “informal” performance improvement plan on me because I’d missed 6 full shifts and left early 4 times in 3 months? He told me flat‑out in his office that if my walking and ladder‑climbing weren’t better by the February 2025 review, they might bump me down to a picker job. Now Carl’s echoing the same thing.

HR’s been breathing down my neck too. Denise emailed on January 6 that I’d already used 9 of my 12 FMLA days for gout since May 2024, and any more full days before May 2025 could go down as unexcused and even mess with my long‑term disability eligibility if my joints give out for good.

All of that is why I keep pushing the doctors to keep these Krystexxa infusions after 2:00 PM, and preferably on Wednesdays or Fridays, so I can work my full 5:00 AM–1:30 PM shift. I can’t afford another late punch‑in because I was stuck in a waiting room.

Sorry this is so heavy. If you’re still up when I get out of the shower, maybe we can look at the calendar together and block off some safe days before the infusion center starts calling.

Love you,
Mike

metadata.subject: Carl’s warning and scheduling these infusions

artifacts.content:
Personal health / work log – updated 01/10/2025

1) Gout / joints
- Chronic tophaceous gout with big tophi over both big toes, Achilles, right elbow. Pain worse on concrete at the warehouse.
- Rheum dx: chronic gouty arthropathy with tophus (M1A.0711). Serum uric acid has been stuck between ~9.2–10.4 mg/dL the last year even on febuxostat.
- Last bad flare: 10/3/24 – right elbow and both feet. Had to crawl from the bedroom to the bathroom at the house on E 83rd. Mom (Karen), who has mild dementia at Prairie Hills Assisted Living, was visiting and panicked, called 911 from the living room. Ended up in Saint Francis ER; bill was ~$1,900, now on 12‑month payment plan.

2) Allergies / med issues
- Allopurinol – February 2020, Dr. Singh at St. Francis. 300 mg daily for ~10 days → diffuse itchy rash, facial swelling. ER overnight; he said “possible Stevens‑Johnson precursor.” Permanently off.
- Colchicine – mid‑2021, Dr. Patel at OU Rheumatology retried 0.6 mg once daily. Terrible nausea, cramping, diarrhea. Missed three days at GreenCountry, lost about $280 OT.
- Lisinopril – dry cough, worst around November 2022. Dr. Harris at Oklahoma Heart Institute ordered chest CT. Woke up coughing 4–5 times a night; Laura slept with earplugs. Switched to losartan and cough stopped.

3) Other health stuff to remember
- Type 2 diabetes creeping up – A1c 9.4% in Jan 2024 per Dr. Chang at Saint Francis. ER 3/2/24 when sugar went over 400 after I stretched insulin to help Emily cover Noah’s $650 emergency dental bill in OKC. Do NOT skip insulin again.
- Depression/anxiety – Laureate since 2023; one inpatient stay Oct 2023 after suicide attempt. Keep Dr. Lopez/Morris in the loop if work stress spikes.

Bring this note (or a copy) to next rheum visit so I don’t forget key dates and names.

metadata.title: Health and work snapshot – gout, meds, other issues

artifacts.content:
Hey Ty,

Catching you up on the medical circus so you don’t just hear bits and pieces from Mom.

The plan now is Krystexxa 8 mg IV every 2 weeks as a change from the pills. Dr. Patel at Hillcrest pushed for it after that November 5, 2025 visit—same week I had that brutal flare while we were watching Noah and Mia overnight. My hands and feet were so bad I couldn’t even pick them up, while you and Em were in the ER in Oklahoma City with Jason for his panic attack. That was a low point.

On top of that, my other stuff isn’t exactly quiet. Depression’s still labeled “major depressive disorder with suicidal ideation” with Dr. Lopez at Laureate; you know I had that inpatient stay back in October 2023. Anxiety flares every time something happens with Grandma Karen. When she moved into Maplewood Assisted Living in February 2021, I ended up at St. John’s ER twice with chest pain that turned out to be panic attacks.

Work’s shaky too. Between July and September 2024 I had five flares bad enough for steroid shots at the Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center. Dr. Hall at South Tulsa Internal Medicine told me at my October 21 visit that if I keep needing high‑dose prednisone, I’ll be in full‑blown diabetes within a year and he’ll have to put me on metformin. Watching Uncle Mark juggle his sugars on the road out of Amarillo, I really don’t want that.

So when they say “STAT” about getting these infusions going, it’s not just doctor talk. It’s my job, my feet, and my head all tied together.

Anyway, enough doom and gloom. How’s the new ticketing system rollout going? Next time my feet cooperate, maybe you can help me figure out how to back up the photos on my phone—I’ve got a ton of Noah’s T‑ball shots I don’t want to lose.

Love,
Dad

metadata.subject: Why they’re finally moving me to the IV gout stuff

artifacts.content:
Event: Krystexxa infusion + labs
Time: Wednesday, January 29, 2026, 2:30–6:00 PM
Location: Tulsa Rheumatology / Infusion Center
Attendees: Mike & Laura

Notes for myself:

1) Timing and work
- Morning shift at GreenCountry: 5:00 AM–1:30 PM. Leave warehouse by 1:40, Laura picks me up out front so I’m not limping across the lot.
- Carl already warned me on 11/12/24 that one more missed start time could cost me the supervisor job, so do NOT schedule anything before 2:00 PM.

2) Labs
- Standing orders are CBC and CMP before first infusion, then every 8 weeks while on Krystexxa, faxed back to Tulsa Rheumatology within 24–48 hours. Because of that fainting spell during the CBC at Saint Francis on 10/3/24, Dr. Patel talked about maybe doing them a little more often at first—every 4 weeks for the first 6 months and extra labs if a flare keeps me out of work more than 2 days.

3) Premeds / infusion details to remember
- 30–60 minutes before: fexofenadine 180 mg PO, acetaminophen 650 mg PO, methylprednisolone 125 mg IV.
- Infuse 8 mg over at least 120 minutes; vitals before, every 30 min, and 60 min after.

4) Scheduling around family stuff
- Avoid first and third Fridays: those are Mom’s dementia check‑ups at Saint Francis and meetings with the Medicaid caseworker about her $4,300 assisted‑living balance.
- Avoid second and fourth Wednesdays in the mornings: I take her from Meadowbrook to the Hillcrest memory clinic at 10:00 and cover $150–$200 in copays.

5) Ride / support
- Emily asked after Noah’s peanut anaphylaxis in August 2024 that I not go alone, so Laura’s coming and needs to be on any reminder texts/emails.

Hi David,

Figured I’d send you one long update instead of a bunch of scattered texts.

The gout is still running the show. Tulsa Ortho Specialists had me in on November 7, 2024. Dr. Karen Patel looked at the tophi over my right Achilles and big toes and said if they’re not aggressively treated in the next 3–6 months, I’m probably headed for joint‑debulking surgery and maybe a right ankle fusion—8–12 weeks totally off my feet. With the $14,000 we blew through last year on Laura’s dental implants at Midtown Family Dentistry, we can’t afford me being out that long, so I’m really leaning into the Krystexxa plan.

Complicating everything is the mental health side. Ever since Mom moved into Maplewood Assisted Living back in February 2021, my anxiety’s been through the roof. I’ve been to St. John’s ER twice for chest pain that ended up being panic attacks. On top of that, I’ve been under Laureate’s care since March 2023 for major depressive disorder with suicidal thoughts, and you know about the inpatient stay in October 2023 after I scared everybody.

Work hasn’t helped. Between July and September 2024 I had five flares bad enough to need steroid shots at the Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center. Dr. Hall told me on October 21 that if I keep doing high‑dose prednisone, my A1c will push me straight into full diabetes and he’ll have to start metformin. I’m already flirting with it—A1c was 9.4% back in January 2024, and I hit the ER at Saint Francis on March 2 when my sugar went over 400 after I stretched my insulin to help Emily cover Noah’s $650 emergency dental bill.

Despite all that, there are still good pockets. Noah’s T‑ball, Mia’s preschool drawings, Ty helping me with the TV when it freezes. Laura and I still daydream about that slow Route 66 drive, just with more rest stops than we used to need.

Call when you get a quiet evening on the road.

Mike

metadata.subject: Orthopedic warning, head stuff, and money stress

artifacts.content:
Finances / medical – running list (updated 09/25/2024)

1) Hospital and clinic bills
- St. Francis ER 10/3/24: Mom called 911 from our living room when she saw me crawling during a gout flare. ER visit ended up being more steroids and pain meds. Total bill ~$1,900; set up 12‑month payment plan, already 2 payments in.
- Gout workups July–September 2024 at Tulsa Rheumatology and St. Francis (labs, imaging): about $3,600 total. On 9/18/24 Laura and I set a 12‑month payment plan with the St. Francis billing office. Already one month behind—watch the mail so it doesn’t go to collections like in 2019.

2) Mom – assisted living and Medicaid
- Karen at Meadowbrook / Prairie Hills (depending which wing they write down). Second and fourth Wednesdays: I take her to Hillcrest memory clinic at 10:00 AM and cover $150–$200 in pharmacy copays.
- Separate issue: overdue assisted‑living balance of ~$4,300. First and third Fridays: drive her to Saint Francis dementia check‑ups and meet with the Medicaid caseworker to sort that balance and her hospice paperwork.

3) Work / legal stuff
- Chapter 13 bankruptcy Laura and I filed May 2024 after falling behind on mortgage and medical bills. Last Friday of each month: court‑mandated financial counseling at Tulsa County Courthouse—must attend or risk dismissal of the case.
- HR at GreenCountry: Denise’s 1/6/25 email about having used 9 of 12 FMLA days for gout since May 2024. Any more full‑day absences before renewal in May 2025 could be unexcused and hurt long‑term disability eligibility.

4) Planning around Krystexxa
- When scheduling infusions: avoid 1st/3rd Fridays (Mom/Medicaid), last Friday (court counseling), and 2nd/4th Wednesday mornings (memory clinic). Keep everything after 2 PM so I can work my 5–1:30 shift.

Keep this note by the computer to check before saying “yes” to any new appointment.
